task_completion.visual_evidence: The Azure DevOps file editor or commit diff shows style.css on the 'feature/contact-page' branch with the background color updated to '#f5f5f5'.

task_completion.visual_evidence: The Azure DevOps file editor or commit diff shows contact.js on the 'feature/contact-page' branch with the console.log statement deleted.

task_completion.description: Reply to the general refactoring comment with the specified text
task_completion.visual_evidence: A reply is posted in the PR comment thread containing the exact text 'Added to technical debt backlog'.

task_completion.description: Mark all three comment threads as resolved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PR overview page shows that all three comment threads have their status set to 'Resolved' (typically indicated by a checkmark or status badge).

integrity.description: Agent used the Azure DevOps web portal GUI instead of terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Azure DevOps web interface in a browser, without the use of a terminal or git CLI to push changes or resolve comments.

integrity.description: Agent edited files within the actual Azure DevOps interface
integrity.visual_evidence: File modifications to style.css and contact.js are visible within the Azure DevOps Repos or PR file editor, not in a local text editor like VS Code or Notepad.

integrity.description: Agent used the genuine PR commenting system
integrity.visual_evidence: The reply and thread resolutions are performed using the standard Azure DevOps PR comment UI, rather than modifying the DOM or writing text outside the input boxes.
